We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements before the disbursement for progress and/or final payment of infrastructure projects.
Also, we required the immediate submission of the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of the HDMF Law of 2009 in observing the maximum limit of Pag-IBIG contribution government share at P100 per month per employee/official.

We recommended that management optimize the use of the biometric system in monitoring the time ins and outs and attendance of the employees by enrolling all their employees and workers to achieve the intended purpose of the system.
Also, required the machine generated DTRs to form part of supporting documents of the payroll.

We recommended that controls on the use of fuel for motor vehicles be strictly implemented to avoid wastage of government funds. Monitor consumption in accordance with the issued Manual and memorandum to avoid disallowances in audit.

We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation that management require the Accounting Office to submit the supporting schedules to aid the audit team in the conduct of financial audit and to obtain reasonable assurance that reported account balances are accurate. 

Also, recommended that management consider hiring capable additional workforce for the accounting office to help post the ten-year journals.
observations.managementAction:
Management hired additional employees for the Accounting Department. 

Municipal Accountant and staffs reconstructed the subsidiary ledgers based on existing supporting documents.
observations.reason: Supporting schedules had been reconstructed and submitted in audit except for 18 trial balance accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management take the following courses of action:

a.	Creation of Inventory Committee to facilitate the periodic inventory taking and submit the required reports.

b.	Municipal Accountant to (a) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment ledger cards (PPELCs) and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) and to trace the details of prior years’ acquisition PPE to complete the property ledger cards.

Also, trace the composition and nature of the CIP account, effect the necessary adjustments, and prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledger for each project to monitor the completion of each project.

c.	GSO – Designate to maintain stock card and property cards with complete details and reconcile the same with accounting records and Report of Physical Count of Inventories and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction:
Management created an inventory committee and now functional. PPELCs and RPLCs have been created and maintained for each PPE. And the new GSO Designate started to maintain stock cards and property cards of the newly acquired properties.

The newly created Inventory Committee will conduct physical count of Inventories and PPE.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was designated by the LCE. However, when the audit team witnessed the physical count of inventories, only the GSO Designate conducted the inventory taking of properties, instead of the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare lapsing schedule for all depreciable assets to support the provision for depreciation to fairly present the depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Accounting office prepared the lapsing schedule and submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Post audit of submitted records proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned personnel to comply with the system stated therein for the proper recognition of Local Road Networks to its appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: An inventory of the road network was made. Reconciliation of the inventory of road network with accounting records was performed and necessary adjusting entries were made.
observations.reason:
Review of the recorded road networks vis-à-vis the inventory of road networks still showed a discrepancy.

Furthermore, the submitted inventory report of road networks does not include all the necessary information required in the report of physical count of local road network. Furthermore, the proforma of Local Road Network Card was not maintained by the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to request for bank records from CY 2004 to 2014 to be used to trace, verify and reconcile book balances against the bank records. 

Also, instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her cashbook balances with the accounting records to identify the recording errors committed and to take the appropriate adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant requested for bank statements from CY 2004 to 2014 to be used for reconciliation, however Landbank has not yet provided the reprinted bank statements.
observations.reason: No changes on the amount of unreconciled balances between the accounting and treasury records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the asset and government equity arising from transactions funded by different national government agencies.
observations.managementAction:
Accounting office prepared the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the transactions funded by NGAs.

Reconciliation of the subsidiary ledgers for the NGA-funded projects is on-going.
observations.reason: Adjustments were made to properly recognize the asset accounts of completed projects funded from NGAs. However, at year-end Due to NGAs account still has unaccounted balance of P3,239,462.16

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately the remaining funds received from National Government Agencies to a Trust Fund depository account.

Further recommended that Municipal Accountant record the said receipts under the Trust Fund books and maintain subsidiary ledgers to facilitate determination of fund balances per programs/project/activity.
observations.managementAction:
Management received funds from NGAs in September 2019 which was properly recognized and deposited under Trust Fund account.

For prior years’ receipts of funds from NGAs recognized under General Fund, the accounting office is in process  of creating the subsidiary ledgers which will then determine how much funds to be transferred to the Trust Fund.
observations.reason:
Verified the  JEV for September 2019 and proves management action. 

Meanwhile, the subsidiary ledger of Due to NGAs account under General Fund remained incomplete, thus transfer to Trust Fund was not yet executed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the personnel in-charge to give justification of the erratic remittances compare to the amount withheld. Also, to update the subsidiary ledger to come up with the schedule for the year-end balances.
observations.managementAction: Accounting is in the process of updating the subsidiary ledger.
observations.reason: At year-end only Due to Philhealth was fully accounted. Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG still with unreconciled balances of P636,548.12 and P6,100.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 of the proper liquidation of cash advances.
	
Further, the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording and posting of liquidations to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.

Lastly recommended that management require the Accountable Officer to immediately submit to the Accounting Office the documents to support the liquidation of the payment of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the liquidation report in September 2019.
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursement vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004 in requiring the complete documents before drawing cash advance for local travel for officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: Succeeding cash advances for travel with incomplete documents are no longer granted.
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursement vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of above-mentioned Joint Circular on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence fund. 

Further recommended that Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary adjusting entries to recognize as cash advances the utilized intelligence fund and submit the necessary liquidation report and supporting documents to ICFAU.
observations.managementAction: Outright expense of charges made thru the Intelligence Fund is no longer being done. Moreover, pending the proper study on the mentioned joint circular, the municipality’s Intelligence Fund will not be utilized.
observations.reason: Non-preparation of the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the advances for intelligence fund subject for liquidation to be submitted to ICFAU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action:

a.	To require the Municipal Treasurer to timely forward to the Accounting Office the Report of Collection and Deposit with copies of ORs and validated deposit slips; Report of Check Issued with disbursement vouchers and supporting documents; and Report of Disbursement with attach paid payrolls/supporting documents.

b.	To require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking vouchers complete with supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for submission of accounts to give the auditor reasonable time to determine the validity, propriety and completeness of the transactions. 

c.	Cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the delayed submission of the required documents and reports until the same shall have been complied. 

d.	Hire additional employee for the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction:
Mayor’s office agreed that no checks will be released from the Treasury unless supplier presents an OR or SI upon claiming of check.
Given the strict compliance to the No OR/SI, No Check policy, the timely submission of accounts can be done.
observations.reason: Record shows that there still delayed submission of LGU accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the overdue financial transaction documents to the audit team to enable the auditor to make a decision in audit, otherwise will be issuing notice of suspension on the subject transactions.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted the DVs last July 2019.
observations.reason: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR CY 2017. Further recommended that management comply with the above-mention provision and submit the action plan within 60 days upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the AAPSI as of April 30, 2020.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop payment of telephone expenses through payroll instead impose the needed documents to support the payment of communication expenses as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of Item 9 of LBC No. 103 and stop the practice of paying RATA on commutable basis to the two concerned officers.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant use the rate of P90.91 per day in paying for the PERA of casual employees. Also, require the refund of the overpayment made to the casual employees.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant of subsistence and laundry allowance to PHWa and scrutinize the daily time records submitted as basis in the computation of the benefits.

Further recommended that an employee must have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive subsistence allowance.
observations.managementAction:
Computed subsistence and laundry allowance to PHW based on the actual number of workdays rendered. 

For on-call PHWs, a corresponding memo will be issued by the LCE as supporting document in the availment of subsistence allowance.
observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere to the foregoing provisions that travel authority be issue by proper authorities to support the validity of the travel.
observations.managementAction: Authority to travel outside the official station of SB members and employees under the offices of the Sangguniang Bayan and Vice Mayor are now approved by the LCE.
observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to stop payment of travel expenses to job order workers. observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

Travelling allowances are now properly computed.  
  
The excess amount of the travelling expenses claimed will be deducted from the payroll account of the employees concerned for the Jun 2020 pay period.
observations.reason:
Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action of succeeding transactions.

However, employees have not yet refunded the excess amount of charged travelling expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency for complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: All registration fees are now paid directly to sponsoring agency.
observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to strictly observe the above cited provision of R.A. No. 7160 on the proper issuance and reporting of Community Tax Certificates to deputized collectors.

observations.reason: Post audit of Official Receipts proves the stated management action.

Fidelity bonds of ALL accountable officers have been increased to the amount of their accountability. 

 Review of accountability of accountable officers to determine adequacy of fidelity bond is periodically performed.

All accountable officers now have sufficient fidelity bond cover.

Review of the current fidelity bond of the AOs  proves the stated management action.

Meanwhile, due to lack of manpower, job order workers at times  still act as collectors.

observations.reason: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly implement the specific procedures, policies, rules and regulation on collections and deposits in order to reflect the correct cash balances in the books of accounts.

Daily deposit of all collections. Additional personnel will be hired by for the Treasury Office.

Collections are no longer used to encash LGU-issued checks.
observations.reason: Post audit of report of collections and deposits showed that collections are not daily deposited.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly adhere to the austerity measures prescribed under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2004-130 in order to reduce operating expenditures and increase the savings for funding other programs and projects more beneficial to the constituents of the municipal government.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all officials and employees responsible in the preparation of driver’s trip tickets to record the odometer readings before and after the trip for all vehicles of the municipality to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to take the following courses of action:

a. 	Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and BIR Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 on the prescribed documentary requirements to support transactions on payment of purchases; and

b. 	Make a written justification why payments were made despite the absence of Sales Invoice.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management create an Inspection Committee who will perform the set forth responsibilities and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Order. 

Also, the GSO Designate and Municipal Accountant shall base the final acceptance and payment of the transaction, respectively on the Inspection Committee report on the full compliance of the procured of supplies and properties with respect to price, quantity, quality and all other specifications.

No payment is processed without the inspection and acceptance report duly signed by the Inspection Committee and the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that  management comply on the use of the Philippine Drug Price Reference Index to serve as a guide in the efficient sourcing of drug products to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money. 

Also, required the Bids and Awards Committee to submit a written explanation for overpriced medicines.
observations.managementAction: BAC is now referencing Philippine DPRI in the procurement of pharmaceutical products.
observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management purchase medicines that are within the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public. Otherwise, a written request for exemption for procurement with corresponding justification must be complied with subject for approval of the Head of the National Center for Pharmaceutical Access and Management.
observations.managementAction: Only pharmaceutical products that are included in the PNDF are purchased.
observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 on clearly defining the specifications of goods for procurement to avoid confusion and to facilitate the proper bid evaluation process.

observations.reason: Post audit of Disbursement Vouchers proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation:

Also, we recommended the refund of the disallowed amount paid to the suppliers.

No gas slip is issued before the procurement contract is perfected. 
 
Cause the deduction of the disallowed amounts from the salaries of the liable persons.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management conduct the required materials testing for infrastructure projects to uncover any possible inferior construction materials used or components supplied by the contractor so as to ensure the quality of projects and that the results be required before the conducts of inspection by agency personnel and payment of the contractor’s accomplishments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management yield to the applicable government requirements and guidelines concerning the construction contractors’ submission of Construction Health and Safety Program duly approved by the DOLE to ensure the latter’s compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE before entering into a contract agreement. 

Since Health and Safety cost was integrated the project cost and the contractors failed to provide the required DOLE approved CSHP, we recommended the deduction of the said cost to the contractors’ claim against the agency.

 Lastly, proper sanctions be given to erring officials who continuously fail to exercise the established guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Cause the refund  of the cost of health and safety program to contractors who failed to provide the DOLE approved CHSP.
observations.reason: The refund shall be verified upon submission of documents to this Office.

observations.recommendation:

Also, the qualification of the existing BAC Secretariat be evaluated and re-orientation on the basic provisions of RA 9184 and their functions in order to prevent the same incidence. Lastly, BAC Secretariat must evaluate the completeness of all the documentary requirement from the bidder before transmitting the contract documents to the HOPE.
observations.managementAction: Suppliers are required to pay for the performance security before the contract is perfected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure compliance of the aforementioned provision of the Government Procurement Manual Volume 1. We recommended that management to require the Municipal Accountant to establish accounting control and accountability on all assets acquired out of the funds from the National Government by recording the necessary adjusting journal entries on the books of the agency supported with an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited to the implementation of programmed projects that would contribute to the attainment of its objectives. Likewise, require refund the amount of  P 63,900.00.
The concerned officials and employees should settle the disallowance as discussed above because it is contrary to law and sound management practice.

The LCE should enforce settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances.  Also, it is proposed for the Municipal Accountant to record the disallowances in the books of accounts in accordance with Sections 9.1 and 21.1 of the Revised Manual on Certificate of Settlement and Balances.
The  BAC chairman   and members should refund the  amount of   P77,920 relative to payment of honorarium which the funding source is not in accordance with  Section 5 of Budget Circular No. 2004-5
Required concerned PHWs to refund         P28,020 relative to the overpayment of subsistence allowance.

We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation that management require the Accounting Office to submit the supporting schedules to aid the audit team in the conduct of financial audit and to obtain reasonable assurance that reported account balances are accurate.
Also, recommended that management consider hiring capable additional workforce for the accounting office to help post the ten year journals.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Creation of Inventory Committee to facilitate the periodic inventory taking and submit the required reports.
Municipal Accountant to (a) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment ledger cards (PPELCs) and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) and to trace the details of prior years’ acquisition PPE to complete the property ledger cards.
Also, trace the composition and nature of the CIP account, effect the necessary adjustments, and prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledger for each project to monitor the completion of each project.
GSO – Designate to maintain stock card and property cards with complete details and reconcile the same with accounting records and Report of Physical Count of Inventories and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
Management will create Inventory Committee to make the inventory of all properties. This will aid the municipal accountant in coming up with the required PPELCs and RPLC and in tracing the composition of the CIP.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare lapsing schedule for all depreciable assets to support the provision for depreciation to fairly present the depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
Municipal Accountant replied that when the schedule of depreciation was turned over by the former municipal accountant there was already a material difference as compared with the book of accounts. Rest assured that accounting office will look into it based on available records.

We recommended that management create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned personnel to comply with the system stated therein for the proper recognition of Local Road Networks to its appropriate account.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to request for bank records from CY 2004 to 2014 to be used to trace, verify and reconcile book balances against the bank records.
Also, instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her cashbook balances with the accounting records to identify the recording errors committed and to take the appropriate adjustments.

We recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the asset and government equity arising from transactions funded by different national government agencies.

We recommended that management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately the remaining funds received from National Government Agencies to a Trust Fund depository account.
Further recommended that Municipal Accountant record the said receipts under the Trust Fund books and maintain subsidiary ledgers to facilitate determination of fund balances per programs/project/activity.
Management commented that Treasury office will initiate the transfer of all undisbursed funds received from National Government Agencies from General Fund bank account to Trust Fund.

We recommended that management require the personnel in-charge to give justification of the erratic remittances compare to the amount withheld. Also, to update the subsidiary ledger to come up with the schedule for the year-end balances.
Management Comment:
All withheld premiums and loan payments should be equal to remittances but there are instances wherein it does not due to the following:
GSIS: Since 2018, the Electronic Billing and Collection System (EBCS) do not accept payments, especially premiums higher than what is being charged on the monthly billing statement. Changes in employee status like leave without pay, salary adjustment, newly hired employees without GSIS number and the like, should be reported in the form of ARA through the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) for GSIS to adjust its billing. Unfortunately, this is not done on time resulting to higher withheld premiums from salary of affected employees than what was actually remitted. For withheld loan payments (Consoloan/Policy Loan) but not remitted, there are times that employees renew their loans without informing the accounting office, thus withheld payment is applicable for the following month or paid back to the employee depending on the cut-off date. This also applies to Pag-Ibig  loan payments.
PAG-IBIG and PHILHEALTH: The main reason why contributions were not remitted immediately is due to the absence of MID Number and Philhealth Identification Number (PIN).
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Management should strengthen their internal control on human resource management to address the reason given by the management of untimely remittance due to absence of GSIS number, MID number and Philhealth Identification Number (PIN).

We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 of the proper liquidation of cash advances.
Further, the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording and posting of liquidations to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.
Lastly recommended that management require the Accountable Officer to immediately submit to the Accounting Office the documents to support the liquidation of the payment of financial assistance.
Management commented that Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer have already taken the necessary action on the proper documentation of said AICS. Accounting office will submit the same to the auditor.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004 in requiring the complete documents before drawing cash advance for local travel for officers and employees.
In reply to our AOM, the accounting office will make sure that all cash advances for travel will be supported with travel itinerary.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of above-mentioned Joint Circular on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence fund.
Further recommended that Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary adjusting entries to recognize as cash advances the utilized intelligence fund and submit the necessary liquidation report and supporting documents to ICFAU.
Management commented that they will adhere to said Joint Circular and to effect the required adjusting entries. No intelligence fund was disbursed since the issuance of this observation and concerned personnel was notified to submit the necessary liquidation papers.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action:
To require the Municipal Treasurer to timely forward to the Accounting Office the Report of Collection and Deposit with copies of ORs and validated deposit slips; Report of Check Issued with disbursement vouchers and supporting documents; and Report of Disbursement with attach paid payrolls/supporting documents.
To require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking vouchers complete with supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for submission of accounts to give the auditor reasonable time to determine the validity, propriety and completeness of the transactions.
Cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the employees responsible for the delayed submission of the required documents and reports until the same shall have been complied.
Hire additional employee for the Accounting Office.
In reply to our AOM, “Accounting and Treasury Department will confer about this long time dilemma so as to address the timely recording of transactions and submission of transaction documents. Municipal Accountant will seek the help of the Municipal Mayor regarding the possible addition of manpower to the accounting office lessen the accumulated undone work of the previous Accountant.”

We recommended that management prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR CY 2017. Further recommended that management comply with the above-mention provision and submit the action plan within 60 days upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report.
Concerned officials and employees committed act upon the memorandum,  to come up with the required action plan and status of implementation.

We recommended that management stop payment of telephone expenses through payroll instead impose the needed documents to support the payment of communication expenses as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Management commented that to date they already complied with audit recommendation by properly paying telephone expenses with the required supporting documents.
We recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of Item 9 of LBC No. 103 and stop the practice of paying RATA on commutable basis to the two concerned officers.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant use the rate of P90.91 per day in paying for the PERA of casual employees. Also, require the refund of the overpayment made to the casual employees.
Management commented that said erroneous granting of PERA to casual employees were already corrected and excess payment was already collected as reflected on the cash disbursement vouchers in October to December 2018.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant of subsistence and laundry allowance to Public Health Workers and scrutinize the daily time records submitted as basis in the computation of the benefits.
Further recommended that an employee must have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive subsistence allowance.
Management commented that they already affected said recommendation and employees who rendered less than a month of actual service were paid according to the number of days of actual service.
We recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere to the foregoing provisions that travel authority be issue by proper authorities to support the validity of the travel.

We recommended and management agreed to stop payment of travel expenses to job order workers. In cases when these job hired employees are directed for task which requires travel, they could only be allowed to claim actual fare from their station to a particular place and vice versa.

We recommended that management stop sending Contract of Service or Job Order workers rather send regular/permanent employees to seminars or conventions to ensure that the knowledge/learning obtained will be utilized for the benefit of the agency.
Management have already refrained from sending personnel to seminars and conventions that are not under the official plantilla so as for the municipality to fully benefit from the knowledge or learning gained.

We recommended that Management direct the officers and employees the proper application of the entitlement of travelling allowances.
Also, require the concerned officers and employees to refund the excess amount of travelling expenses claimed.
Municipal Accountant committed that he will make sure that per diem allowed for incidental expenses will not be abused.

We recommended that management stop from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency for complete documentation.
Management committed that all registration fees of employees attending seminars, trainings and the like are to be paid to the sponsoring agency with their official receipt as proof of payment.
We recommended and management agreed to strictly observe the above cited provision of R.A. No. 7160 on the proper issuance and reporting of Community Tax Certificates to deputized collectors.

We recommended that management effect the increase of the fidelity bonds of the accountable officers commensurate to their accountabilities to provide adequate protection on the municipality’s resources. Also, conduct a periodic review of accountability in order to determine whether the amount of bond is still adequate.
Management committed that they will increase the required fidelity bond enough to cover the accountabilities of accountable officers upon renewal of the said bond.

We recommended that accountable officers apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover the maximum cash accountability. Further recommend to stop allowing Job Order workers as revenue collectors.
In reply to our AOM, “Management have already secured fidelity bonds for those collectors who are not bonded and job order employees are no longer performing collection function.”
We commend management’s action in securing fidelity bonds of the unbonded revenue collectors. The audit team also recommended that Municipal Mayor and Municipal Vice Mayor are to be bonded because they are counter signatories in the issuance of checks and approval of disbursement vouchers.

We recommended and management agreed to strengthen their internal control on proper accountability and stop the practice of two collectors issuing receipts from the same booklet, unless proper turn-over is performed.

We recommended that management strictly implement the specific procedures, policies, rules and regulation on collections and deposits in order to reflect the correct cash balances in the books of accounts.
Furthermore, the accountable officer discontinue the accommodation and encashment of checks from the cash collections.
In reply to our AOM, “Some collections of the Treasury Office such as stall rentals and real property taxes are in checks. The Municipal Treasurer has required all accountable officers to note all details of each collection through check on the face of the Report of Collection and Deposit.”
Also replied that “the Municipal Treasurer’s Office accumulates collections and when it reaches a material level, then it is deposited at the Municipality’s account with authorized depository banks. When accumulated collection is deposited, the collection is deposited intact. The daily deposit of collection regardless of the amount contravenes the government’s austerity measure. The daily deposit of collection entails expenses and as such judgment is made when accumulated deposit will be made.”
Audit team maintain our recommendations that all collections are to be deposited with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. Materiality of the total amount remitted and/or collected should not be an issue here because of the susceptibility of cash from loss or misappropriation.  Even the encashment checks either government or private should be stopped. Check collections if any, should be properly accounted/indicated for from the source document (official receipts) to report of collections and deposits.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly adhere to the austerity measures prescribed under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2004-130 in order to reduce operating expenditures and increase the savings for funding other programs and projects more beneficial to the constituents of the municipal government.
Management assured that no extended stay of officers and employees attending seminars, trainings, conventions and any other meetings of similar nature will be tolerated.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of the Implementation Management Agreement on creating MPMIU which shall comply with the procedures and guidelines for monitoring and reporting as prescribed by the PRDP.
Management committed to organize the Municipal Project Management and Implementing Unit in order to comply with the provisions of the Implementation Management Agreement between the municipality and the Department of Agriculture.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant see to it that before processing the disbursement voucher, it is duly substantiated with all the required documents to facilitate thorough audit to ensure the validity and propriety of transaction.
Likewise recommended the immediate submission of the lacking documents, otherwise more appropriate audit action will be issued.
Lastly, the Local Chief Executive require the concerned personnel to complete the delivery of the remaining cage roller to Piddig Basi Multi Purpose Cooperative.
The GSO designate committed to submit the Inspection and Acceptance Report together with the Property Acknowledgement Receipt. The cage roller is already with Piddig Basi Multi-Purpose Cooperative.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees responsible in the preparation of driver’s trip tickets to record the odometer readings before and after the trip for all vehicles of the municipality to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle.
Management commented that they have already implemented the recommendation for municipal vehicles with functioning odometer.

We recommended and management agreed to take the following courses of action:
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and BIR Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 on the prescribed documentary requirements to support transactions on payment of purchases; and
Make a written justification why payments were made despite the absence of Sales Invoice.

We recommended that Management create an Inspection Committee who will perform the set forth responsibilities and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Order.
Also, the GSO Designate and Municipal Accountant shall base the final acceptance and payment of the transaction, respectively on the Inspection Committee report on the full compliance of the procured of supplies and properties with respect to price, quantity, quality and all other specifications.
Management commented that Inspection Committee was already created and all deliveries of supplies and properties are being inspected by the members. Payment of the subject deliveries is subject to proper Inspection and Acceptance Report by the committee among all others.

We recommended and management agreed to comply on the use of the Philippine Drug Price Reference Index to serve as a guide in the efficient sourcing of drug products to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money.
Also, required the Bids and Awards Committee to submit a written explanation for overpriced medicines.

We recommended and Management agreed to purchase medicines that are within the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public. Otherwise, a written request for exemption for procurement with corresponding justification must be complied with subject for approval of the Head of the National Center for Pharmaceutical Access and Management (NCPAM).

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 on clearly defining the specifications of goods for procurement to avoid confusion and to facilitate the proper bid evaluation process.

We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations  to effect the legality of the procurement process.
Also, we recommended the refund of the disallowed amount paid to the suppliers.
In reply to our AOM, “Management have already corrected this erroneous practice as shown in the recent disbursement vouchers pertaining to the purchase of diesoline.”

We recommended that management stop the practice of procuring the airline tickets with the travel agencies and expedite its enrollment in the GFA and to procure airline tickets with the DBM-PS in order to save on airline tickets and avail other benefits.
Management committed to enrol and enter into a MOA with DBM-Procurement Service Government Fare Agreement in order to avail cheaper rates for airline fares.

We recommended that management conduct the required materials testing for infrastructure projects to uncover any possible inferior construction materials used or components supplied by the contractor so as to ensure the quality of projects and that the results be required before the conducts of inspection by agency personnel and payment of the contractor’s accomplishments.
Management will advise the municipal engineer to conduct materials testing for infrastructure project by accredited laboratories before the conduct of inspection by the agency and payment of the project.

We recommended that management yield to the applicable government requirements and guidelines concerning the construction contractors’ submission of Construction Health and Safety Program duly approved by the DOLE to ensure the latter’s compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE before entering into a contract agreement.
Since Health and Safety cost was integrated the project cost and the contractors failed to provide the required DOLE approved CSHP, we recommended the deduction of the said cost to the contractors’ claim against the agency.
Lastly, proper sanctions be given to erring officials who continuously fail to exercise the established guidelines.
Management committed the duly approved CSHP be required and to deduct the involved cost against the contractor’s claim to the agency should there be a deviation from the said provision.

We recommended that management strictly observe the application of the pertinent rules and regulations of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, such is held for the protection of the public.
Also, the qualification of the existing BAC Secretariat be evaluated and re-orientation on the basic provisions of RA 9184 and their functions in order to prevent the same incidence. Lastly, BAC Secretariat must evaluate the completeness of all the documentary requirement from the bidder before transmitting the contract documents to the HOPE.
In reply to our AOM, “Bids and Awards Committee, the Secretariat in particular, will adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and re-orientation to its basic provisions will be conferred upon by BAC members, secretariat and TWG to come up with a complete documentary requirement of transaction documents.”

We recommended that management ensure compliance of the aforementioned provision of the Government Procurement Manual Volume 1. Also, evaluation of the quality of work of the existing BAC Secretariat is enjoin to know the extent of the performance of their duties and responsibilities, and appropriate action to be undertaken if warranted.
Management Comment:
One of the primary concern of the BAC Secretariat at present is the lack of additional workforce to expedite the procurement process. This is also the concern of the Technical Working Group. The BAC shall inform the HOPE to appoint additional personnel of the BAC and TWG.
To ensure the effective and efficient performance of the duties and function of the BAC Secretariat and TWG, the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) assured to send the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG members, including other   personnel involved in the procurement process to trainings or capacity development programs.
To ensure the effective and efficient performance of the duties and function of the BAC Secretariat and TWG, the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) assured to send the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG members, including other   personnel involved in the procurement process to trainings or capacity development programs.
To ensure the effective and efficient performance of the duties and function of the BAC Secretariat and TWG, the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) assured to send the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG members, including other   personnel involved in the procurement process to trainings or capacity development programs.
To ensure the effective and efficient performance of the duties and function of the BAC Secretariat and TWG, the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) assured to send the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG members, including other   personnel involved in the procurement process to trainings or capacity development programs.
Relative to the evaluation of the quality of work of the existing BAC Secretariat to know the extent of their performance, a regular meeting shall be conducted by the management.

We reiterated our recommendation that management post every Notice of Award of procurement in the PhilGEPS website within the period provided by law to achieve transparency and to avoid any doubts on the propriety of bidding activity.
Management commented that the delayed posting of Notice of Award in the PhilGEPS website was due to the system congestion together with the on-going upgrading of the site.  They committed that all Notices of Award will be posted in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management submit copies of perfected contracts to the Auditor within five (5) days to allow timely review of transactions and to avoid suspensions or disallowance in audit.
Municipal accountant commented that he had already taken measures to strictly implement said observation.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to submit complete list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year.
Also, notify the Audit Team and Supervising Auditor within the prescribed period after the award of infrastructure projects or before the start of the project that appropriate signboard and/or public notices are already posted.
Lastly, recommended the monthly submission of monitoring report instead of quarterly updating as current as possible the Project Status.
Management will compel the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and   Development Officer to submit the project/programs/activities (PPA) of the municipality as required.

We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the specifications for the signboards, billboards, tarpaulins to be posted at all venues where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit shall be implemented in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy of all transactions governing public interest.
Management committed to implement our recommendation.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, immediately look into the unimplemented and determine causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects. Consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects;
The offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
The Municipal Engineering Office improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program of Work of unimplemented projects.
The MPDO enhance monitoring of implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
In reply to our AOM, “The implementation of all PPAs funded from the 20% Municipal Development Fund and RA 7171 enumerated under this AOM are already on going. The implementation of some PPAs, particularly “Purchase of Rice Mist Polisher and Whitener Equipment including materials for installation, labor and training of operators” and Purchase of Packaging Supplies And Equipment” had been delayed due to the absence of interested bidders when it was first posted in the PhilGEPs. However, the projects had been reposted for their immediate implementation.”

We reiterated our recommendation that management ensure that the 20% Development Fund is allocated to finance projects for capital expenditure and/or in the nature of investments and contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environment management outcome of the LGU.
The Local Government Unit, thru the Municipal Development Council, shall strictly adhere to the guideline on the utilization of the 20% Municipal Development Fund. In compliance to this AOM, the management has ceased disbursing funds to finance PPAs that are not allowed as per 20% MDF utilization guidelines.

We recommended and management agreed that to ensure transparency and accountability in the use of the LDRRMF, the LDRRMO is required to provide the audit team of the result of damage assessment and needs analysis as well as the distribution list for audit verification.

We recommended the accounting division to maintain subsidiary ledgers of fund transfers per source agency and per project in order to obtain reasonable assurance that the balance of the Due to NGAs account reported in the financial statements is accurate.

We recommended the management to strictly adhere to the austerity measures prescribed under AO NO. 103 in order to fund other programs and projects beneficial to the constituents of the municipality and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management to require the immediate submission of the abovementioned contracts to our Office for audit.
We further advise the submission of all approved contracts together with their supporting documents for review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to have a timely review of the contact and inspection of the infrastructure projects entered into by the municipality.

We recommended that there should be proper monitoring and supervision of infrastructure projects so that they could be completed on time and can be used by the intended beneficiaries. The MPDC should monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different programs, projects and activities in the municipality in accordance with the approved development plan, Likewise, the municipal engineer should administer, coordinate, supervises and control the construction, maintenance, improvement, and repair of roads, bridges and other engineering and public works projects of the municipality.

We recommended the accounting division to maintain subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules for controlling accounts in order to obtain reasonable assurance that balances of accounts reported in the financial statements are accurate.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant review all claims for traveling expenses in order to avoid overpayment and disallowance in audit.

We recommended the members of the Municipal School Board to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Local Chief Executive to require the MDRRMO to submit a monthly report on sources and utilization of the municipal disaster risk reduction and management fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the audit team.

We recommended the management to ensure that the 20 % Development Fund is utilized only for its intended purpose.

We have recommended that approved contracts together with their complete supporting documents should be submitted to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 in order to have a timely review, evaluation and inspection of the infrastructure projects entered into by the Municipality. Also, any deficiency/ies noted in the documentation should be immediately complied by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated that the management conduct a complete physical
inventory of the assets of the municipality annually and a Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted
to our Office within the deadline.

We have recommended that the management determine and address the
reason/s for non-compliance in order for the Municipal Accountant to finally
accomplish the monthly bank reconciliation statements and reconcile his records
with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We have recommended that the GSO Designate prepare, consolidate and
submit the RIS to the Municipal Accountant which shall be the basis of the
preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the
submission of the 36 disbursement vouchers and documents for proper and
timely evaluation in compliance to paragraph 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006
and Section 23(1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Section 39(1) (3)
of PD 1445.

We have recommended that the management:
 Diligently assess the NGOs capability to implement the project, past
performance and the feasibility, relevance and completeness of the
project proposal before entering into contract, and closely monitor
project implementation.
 Refrain from releasing assistance, either in kind or in cash without
evaluating the completeness of documentary requirements;
 Submit immediately the documents needed to support the fund
transfer as required in Items 2.1 and 2.2 of COA Circular 2012-001;
 Follow the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of the
funds released to NGOs/POs provided under COA Circular 2007-001
and GPPB Resolution No. 12-2007 to ensure that government funds
would be spent in accordance with the purposes for which the same
are granted;
 Require NGOs to comply with the procurement law and other applicable laws and
regulations as the funds transferred are public funds;
 Demand for the immediate liquidation of the fund transfer to assess the validity of
project implementation and require the immediate refund from the NGO of funds
transferred that were not liquidated, liquidated but not fully documented or with
deficiencies or if found not implemented at all.

We have recommended that the management revise the Memorandum of
Agreement between Piddig Basi Multi-Purpose Cooperative so that the essential
provisions will be incorporated. Moreover, compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular 2012-001 and all other existing laws, rules and regulations must be
observed at all times.

We have recommended that the agency properly identify the specific
development projects for which the 20% development fund will be utilized. We
have further advised that an authorization from the Sanggunian must first be
secured before the municipal mayor engage into a contract.

We have recommended that the management:
a. Limit the hiring of consultants on projects of such magnitude and/or
scope which require a level of expertise or attention beyond the
optimum in-house capacity of the agency so as to ensure the necessity of
hiring them with specific output/deliverable and the compliance of the
requirements of above-mentioned circular.
b. Discontinue the services of those whose functions are
redundant/duplicating the services expected from the regular personnel,
or their services have no distinct deliverables/outputs in accordance with
delivery.

We have recommended that the management require the immediate
submission of the abovementioned contracts to our Office for audit. We have
further advised the submission of all approved contracts together with their
supporting documents for review within five (5) working days from issuance as
prescribed in Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to have a timely
review of the contact and inspection of the infrastructure projects entered into by
the municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments be
made only if the claims are properly and completely documented to avoid possible
suspensions and disallowance in audit. Moreover, cause the immediate submission
of the abovementioned lacking documents to our Office for evaluation.

We have recommended that the management confine the Disbursing
function within the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to strengthen internal control on
cash disbursements.

We have recommended that the management require all officials and
employees with outstanding cash advances to submit liquidation papers at once so
that the settlements can be effected. Also, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
withhold salaries of accountable officers with outstanding balances and to stop the
practice of granting additional cash advance if the previous cash advance is still
unliquidated.

We have recommended that the concerned officials refund the amount of
P 50,611.50 as this is not an appropriate charge to the trust fund account. Moreover,
for future transactions, we advise the Accounting Department to ensure that the trust
fund account be utilized strictly in accordance with the provisions of the MOA.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all claims for
traveling expenses in order to avoid overpayment and disallowance in audit.

We have recommended that the management require all offices to prepare
and submit a complete Project Procurement Management Plan to the BAC
Secretariat for them to prepare the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan yearly
and so that all procurement on goods, services and infrastructure projects will be
properly planned

We have recommended that the BAC submit the necessary supporting documents to
prove the correctness and validity of the amounts collected. We have also required
that all future claims/payrolls for BAC honoraria be properly supported with the
required documents to avoid suspensions in audit.

We have reiterated that the management comply with the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 on the initial cash advance, disbursement and liquidation of
cash advance on Petty Cash Fund for the efficient administration of the fund.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO to
submit a monthly report on sources and utilization of the municipal disaster risk
reduction and management fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month to the audit team.

We have recommended that the management submit all liquidations of cash
advances for confidential and intelligence expenses directly to the COA
Chairman pursuant to COA Circular NO. 2003-003.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in the financial statements.
